question_text: Compare the energy stores of two identical balls: Ball X is held 2 m above the ground, and Ball Y is held 1 m above the ground but is moving at 4 m/s. Explain which ball has more total mechanical energy and why. (Use g = 10 m/s², mass = 1 kg for both)
correct_answer: Ball X has more total mechanical energy (20 J) compared to Ball Y (18 J). Ball X: E_p = 1 × 10 × 2 = 20 J, E_k = 0 J (stationary). Ball Y: E_p = 1 × 10 × 1 = 10 J, E_k = ½ × 1 × 4² = 8 J. Total for X = 20 J; Total for Y = 18 J.
explanation: To compare total mechanical energy, we must calculate both gravitational potential energy and kinetic energy for each ball. Ball X has more height but no movement, while Ball Y has less height but is moving. When we add up all the energy stores, Ball X has 20 J total while Ball Y has only 18 J total, making Ball X have more total mechanical energy.
